title: provide support for signedIdentity: matchRepository
body:
Hi, 

I'm currently trying to mirror a cosign signed container to a private registry and enforce signature checks there also. 

for that I'm doing in `policy.json`

```
            "my-private-registry": [
                {
                   "type": "sigstoreSigned",
                   "keyPath": "/usr/share/pki/containers/suse-container-key.pem",
                   "signedIdentity": {"type": "remapIdentity",
                       "signedPrefix": "registry.suse.com/suse",
                       "prefix": "my-private-registry/dirkmueller"
                   }
                }
            ]
```

this however fails with ` Source image rejected: Signature for identity "registry.suse.com/suse/git" is not accepted`. The cosign signature identity is "registry.suse.com/suse/git", which is being rejected as `matchRepoDigestOrExact` semantics are applied. We cannot really sign on the originating registry with a digest or exact, so I'm looking for a way to specify the matching type to `matchRepository`

From a brief look at the code, this doesn't seem to be possible. can it please be added? I've noticed that cosign --verify is ignoring the identity alltogether and accepts any (this is documented in the spec https://github.com/sigstore/cosign/blame/544d76a7098565aea9a77ab8e9ca60e5ea825d82/specs/SIGNATURE_SPEC.md#L268 ).
